Question: Claims for a refund of an overcharge in respect of goods carried by a railway or to compensation for the loss, destruction or deterioration of goods delivered to it should be made to the railway administration within _________ from the date of the delivery of the goods for carriage by railway.

A: The Administrative authorities should check up the claim with reference to the relevant records

Question: The period of limitation of Gratuity is ________ from the date when the claim fell due against Government for payment.

A: when money due to a local body has been credited to General Revenues by a mistake on the part of a Government Officer

C: as per the discretion of the insurance company

A: The Inspecting officer will compare the entries in the counterfoils in the printed counterfoil receipts book with those in the register of collections and with any original receipt they may be able to obtain from the persons to whom they were issued
B: The officer will check the totals in the Register of collections and will see that the amount shown as collected agrees with the total of the amounts paid into the Treasury as shown in the chalan and the cash in the hands of the Government servant

Question: Every executive authority of a Municipal Council or City Corporation and Village Officer should report immediately to the disbursing officer concerned the death of any person who was residing within his jurisdiction and drawing a Government pension, whether civil military, political or of any other kind
C: as per the discretion of the executive authority

C: as per the discretion of civil aviation department
